zh.Saint Donats, Glamorganshire, Wales: 威爾士格拉莫爾根聖 堂納次
zh.Saint Donats, The Vale of Glamorgan, Wal: 英國威爾斯瓦爾 本 格拉莫爾根聖 堂納次
zh.lifeSummary: Thomas de Halweia是N. N. Halweia和N. N.的兒子，於1248年出生在英國威爾斯瓦爾 本 格拉莫爾根聖 堂納次。他於大約1282年與unknown在英國英格蘭薩默塞特結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Thomas de Halweia是N. N. Halweia和N. N.的儿子，于1248年出生在英国威尔斯瓦爾 本 格拉莫爾根聖 堂納次。他于大约1282年与unknown在英国英格兰薩默塞特结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

en.lifeSummary: Thomas de Halweia was born in 1248, in Saint Donats, Vale of Glamorgan, Wales, United Kingdom as the son of N. N. Halweia and N. N.. He married unknown about 1282, in Somerset, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter.
